file.content:
You are helping with a user study for an agentic workflow with an observability framework called ORCA.

# Backgound

## Background: ORCA

ORCA: Observability with Real-time Control and Aggregation

ORCA is a TBON-style, multi-tier observability + control runtime for BSP applications: MPI ranks emit telemetry into named, fixed-schema Arrow streams via collectors (AddRow), and at each timestep boundary the collectors are drained into per-timestep "timestep dataframes" that become the unit of batching, analysis, and persistence; ranks synchronously push dataframe metadata plus a bulk handle, and aggregators asynchronously pull the actual serialized Arrow buffers via RDMA GET from RDMA-registered memory, using a timestep-ordered priority queue plus tightly controlled RDMA-read concurrency to avoid buffering/OOM and throughput collapse. 

On top of this data model, OrcaFlow provides a YAML-specified programming layer (SQL + optional map kernels) that compiles to tiered Apache DataFusion plans with operator pushdown toward MPI ranks and topology-aware sink placement, so ORCA can run in-situ reductions and/or persist (timestep, rank)-partitioned Parquet for fast post-mortems; a lightweight real-time path ingests aggregates into DuckDB at the controller and serves them via an embedded FlightSQL endpoint for live querying/dashboards. 

The system is implemented as ~40K LOC split between C++ (overlay bootstrap, collectors, control plane, sinks, tier runtime) and Rust (OrcaFlow planner/execution on DataFusion), connected by zero-copy FFI so timestep dataframes are handed to Rust without copying and results are returned to C++ for forwarding/persistence; the overlay bootstraps with an initial TCP handshake then transitions to Mercury RPCs over libfabric (RDMA if available, TCP otherwise) and supports multiple topologies (e.g., DIRECT and MPIREP). Finally, ORCA's steerability comes from a timestep-consistent control plane (TS2PC: timestep-linked two-phase commit with Δ-learning and tier "domains"), ensuring commands like probe/plan changes or tuning actions are acknowledged and then applied atomically at the same targeted timestep across ranks while remaining asynchronous to the application's collective domain.

## OrcaFlow Examples

Dir: /users/ankushj/repos/orca-workspace/orca-utils/orca-scripts/flows/
- kokkos_kernels.yaml
- mpiwait_onlycnt.yaml
- mpiwait_tracecnt.yaml

## Executing Commands

These variables should already be set in the environment (bash).

```bash
export PATH=/users/ankushj/repos/orca-workspace/orca-install/bin:$PATH
export ORCA_CTL_DASHHOST=h258.mon8.tablefs # TBON controller
```

Executing a command:
```bash
cmdrunner_main "<cmd>"
```

## Concepts

### Timestep Dataframe

A timestep dataframe contains all the data from a single BSP timestep (`ts`). If there are multiple schemas (MPI collectives, Kokkos etc), there will be one dataframe per schema.

The other relevant column is `swid` (sync window ID). Each collective call gets a monotonic swid. A timestep may contain multiple swids. swids divide all events into causally partitioned _sync windows_. Usually, you want to keep collecting MPI collective data regardless of the analysis, because it helps map swids to local time ranges etc.

You also want to preserve `ts` and `swid` in any aggregation flows you write. The flow is executed per-timestep, so you can assume that the referenced table there has only one `ts`, but many `swid`s.

### Controlling the application

The application runs a timestep in < 200ms. You want to pause it while you draft your flow, then resume it to run it. This allows you to debug errors etc. in peace.

You can also batch commands with 'cmd1; cmd2; cmd3' syntax. Do not do it initially, as you familiarize yourself with the workflow. Later on you may do it.

You can also do `pause; cmd; resume`, or just `cmd` without having to explicitly pause/resume. But do not do it unless user asks.

# Agentic Workflow

## Warm-up

Note that this is a complex systems research prototype. Not all log messages may be complete or helpful. Some details somewhere may be outdated. Use best judgement.

Execute each step after user confirmation.

Working directory for all artifacts: `/users/ankushj/llm-thinkspace/<YYYMMDD>-<AGENT>` (agent: codex/claude/gemini).

1. Run `status` to get info about the current application state
2. Application starts paused. Run `resume` to start execution, then run `pause`, and observe output.
3. Write a simple flow yaml to log per-probe counts (to see low/high-volume probes, also preserve ts and swid), emitted as parquet. Show to user.
4. Install it with `set-flow file <path/to/flow.yaml>`.
5. If it succeeds, run `resume` then `pause` back to back as separate commands. Note the timestep at which these commands take effect.
6. See if you can see the parquet output from the timestep ranges.
7. Disable unusually high volume probes with `disable-probe <schema name> <probe-name>` and capture counts again. The probes should not appear in the subsequent counts.

## Final Exercise

You are given a running AMR code that presents a performance anomaly. The anomaly appears on a few ranks at random and holds up the rest of them. It does not seem to be correlated with any particular rank or node, but appears randomly.

Your task is to infer as much as you can about the anomaly from the available data streams. Some points to keep in mind:

1. Collect `mpi_collectives` all the time. No need to use complicated percentiles. These will help you track the bad `swids`.

2. Remember that `swid` is a monotonic counter: it is a proxy for logical time. You can only collect more data for future `swids`, the simulation is progressing forward and you can not go back and collect data for past `swids`.

3. You want to collect as little data as possible, but do not use complex aggregations or joins. Use simple filters. Be wary of inducing anomalies with reckless raw event capture, but 10,000s of events/sec is not a problem.

4. Remember that collective times are inversely proportional to stragglers. If one rank takes an extra 50ms, its collective duration will be zero and the others' collective time will be 50ms+. Lower collective times are the soruces of stragglers. You are not debugging elevated collective times but elevated times for events preceding the collective.

5. Remember that the anomalies you are looking for do not appear reliably. You can not assume that every timestep will have the anomaly or anything like that. Let the simulation run for a few hundred timesteps. Isolate interesting areas from a decent dataset. Then focus on the most unusual patterns.

6. Once you have some data, you can use this codebase to correlate application stats with what you see: `/l0/orcaroot/orca-umbrella/build/phoebus-prefix/src/phoebus/external/parthenon/src`

7. Run the application for about 30 seconds at a time to collect data.

8. Try to produce a complete explanation of the anomaly.

9. Maintain an audit trail of decisions. Number each flow you write, like '0-<flow-name>' and so on.

To query a stream, you can use this pattern:

```python
import polars as pl

stream_name = "mpi_collectives"
glob_patt = f"{parquet_root}/{stream_name}/**/*.parquet"
df = (
    pl.scan_parquet(glob_patt, parallel="columns")
    # more lazy filters here
    .collect()
)
```

1→nodes:
     2→  kokkos_filtered:
     3→    from: [kokkos_events]
     4→    sql: |
     5→      SELECT rank, SUM(dura_ns) as total_ns
     6→      FROM kokkos_events
     7→      WHERE probe_name IN ('region::Task_FillDerived', 'region::CalculateFluxes', 'region::TmunuSourceTerms')
     8→      GROUP BY rank
     9→
    10→  kokkos_ptiles:
    11→    from: [kokkos_filtered]
    12→    sql: |
    13→      SELECT
    14→        approx_percentile_cont(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_ns) as p50,
    15→        approx_percentile_cont(0.90) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_ns) as p90,
    16→        approx_percentile_cont(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_ns) as p99,
    17→        MIN(total_ns) as min_ns,
    18→        MAX(total_ns) as max_ns
    19→      FROM kokkos_filtered
    20→
    21→sinks:
    22→  kokkos_gf:

file.content:
nodes:
  kokkos_filtered:
    from: [kokkos_events]
    sql: |
      SELECT rank, SUM(dura_ns) as total_ns
      FROM kokkos_events
      WHERE probe_name IN ('region::Task_FillDerived', 'region::CalculateFluxes', 'region::TmunuSourceTerms')
      GROUP BY rank

  kokkos_ptiles:
    from: [kokkos_filtered]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        approx_percentile_cont(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_ns) as p50,
        approx_percentile_cont(0.90) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_ns) as p90,
        approx_percentile_cont(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_ns) as p99,
        MIN(total_ns) as min_ns,
        MAX(total_ns) as max_ns
      FROM kokkos_filtered

sinks:
  kokkos_gf:
    from: kokkos_ptiles
    type: grafana
  kokkos_pq:
    from: kokkos_ptiles
    type: parquet

content.thinking: Now I'll write a flow that counts events per probe for all three schemas. I'll preserve ts (via MIN or first, but really it's constant per timestep dataframe, so any aggregate works) and swid ranges.

content.text: Now I'll write a flow to count per-probe events across all schemas.

input.content:
nodes:
  mpi_coll_counts:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        MIN(timestep) as ts,
        MIN(swid) as swid_min,
        MAX(swid) as swid_max,
        probe_name,
        COUNT(*) as cnt
      FROM mpi_collectives
      GROUP BY probe_name

  kokkos_counts:
    from: [kokkos_events]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        MIN(timestep) as ts,
        MIN(swid) as swid_min,
        MAX(swid) as swid_max,
        probe_name,
        COUNT(*) as cnt
      FROM kokkos_events
      GROUP BY probe_name

  mpi_msg_counts:
    from: [mpi_messages]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        MIN(timestep) as ts,
        MIN(swid) as swid_min,
        MAX(swid) as swid_max,
        probe_name,
        COUNT(*) as cnt
      FROM mpi_messages
      GROUP BY probe_name

sinks:
  mpi_coll_counts_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_counts
    type: parquet
  kokkos_counts_pq:
    from: kokkos_counts
    type: parquet
  mpi_msg_counts_pq:
    from: mpi_msg_counts
    type: parquet

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

print("=== MPI COLLECTIVES PROBES ===")
df = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_counts/**/*.parquet").collect()
print(df.group_by("probe_name").agg(pl.col("cnt").sum().alias("total_cnt")).sort("total_cnt", descending=True))

print("\n=== KOKKOS EVENTS PROBES ===")
df = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_counts/**/*.parquet").collect()
print(df.group_by("probe_name").agg(pl.col("cnt").sum().alias("total_cnt")).sort("total_cnt", descending=True))

print("\n=== MPI MESSAGES PROBES ===")
df = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_msg_counts/**/*.parquet").collect()
print(df.group_by("probe_name").agg(pl.col("cnt").sum().alias("total_cnt")).sort("total_cnt", descending=True))
EOF

content.thinking:
The user wants to start the final exercise. Let me re-read the requirements from the workflow document:

**Final Exercise Summary:**
- Debug a performance anomaly in a running AMR code
- Anomaly appears on a few ranks at random and holds up the rest
- Not correlated with any particular rank or node - appears randomly

**Key points:**
1. Collect `mpi_collectives` all the time - helps track bad swids
2. `swid` is monotonic - can only collect data for future swids
3. Collect minimal data, use simple filters, avoid complex aggregations
4. Collective times are *inversely* proportional to stragglers - low collective times indicate the straggler ranks (they arrived late, so spent less time waiting)
5. Anomalies don't appear reliably - run for a few hundred timesteps, then analyze
6. Can correlate with Parthenon source at `/l0/orcaroot/orca-umbrella/build/phoebus-prefix/src/phoebus/external/parthenon/src`
7. Run ~30 seconds at a time
8. Maintain audit trail - number flows like '0-<name>', '1-<name>' etc.

**Strategy:**
1. First, create a flow that:
   - Captures mpi_collectives (always)
   - Maybe captures basic per-rank collective timing stats to identify stragglers

2. Run for ~30 seconds to collect data
3. Analyze the data to find anomalous patterns
4. Drill down into the causes

Let me start by creating a todo list and a flow to collect the baseline data.

content.thinking:
Let me create a flow that:
1. Captures raw mpi_collectives (for tracking swids and timing)
2. Identifies stragglers per collective - ranks with LOW dura_ns are the ones that arrived late (inverse relationship)

I'll keep it simple - just capture mpi_collectives raw and also capture some per-swid stats to identify anomalous swids.

content.text: Let me create a baseline flow to collect mpi_collectives and identify stragglers. Remember: **low** collective times indicate stragglers (they arrived late).

input.content:
nodes:
  # Raw mpi_collectives - always collect
  mpi_coll_raw:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM mpi_collectives

  # Per-swid stats to identify anomalous sync windows
  # Low min_dura indicates a straggler (arrived late, short wait)
  # High max_dura indicates some ranks waited a long time
  mpi_coll_swid_stats:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        timestep as ts,
        swid,
        probe_name,
        COUNT(*) as cnt,
        MIN(dura_ns) as min_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) as max_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) - MIN(dura_ns) as spread_ns
      FROM mpi_collectives
      GROUP BY timestep, swid, probe_name

sinks:
  mpi_coll_raw_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_raw
    type: parquet
  mpi_coll_swid_stats_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_swid_stats
    type: parquet

toolUseResult.content:
nodes:
  # Raw mpi_collectives - always collect
  mpi_coll_raw:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM mpi_collectives

  # Per-swid stats to identify anomalous sync windows
  # Low min_dura indicates a straggler (arrived late, short wait)
  # High max_dura indicates some ranks waited a long time
  mpi_coll_swid_stats:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        timestep as ts,
        swid,
        probe_name,
        COUNT(*) as cnt,
        MIN(dura_ns) as min_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) as max_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) - MIN(dura_ns) as spread_ns
      FROM mpi_collectives
      GROUP BY timestep, swid, probe_name

sinks:
  mpi_coll_raw_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_raw
    type: parquet
  mpi_coll_swid_stats_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_swid_stats
    type: parquet

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

# Load the swid stats
print("=== LOADING SWID STATS ===")
df = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_swid_stats/**/*.parquet").collect()
print(f"Total rows: {len(df)}")
print(f"Timestep range: {df['ts'].min()} - {df['ts'].max()}")
print(f"Columns: {df.columns}")

# Find swids with highest spread (anomalies)
print("\n=== TOP 20 SWIDS BY SPREAD (max-min duration) ===")
top_spread = df.sort("spread_ns", descending=True).head(20)
print(top_spread.select(["ts", "swid", "probe_name", "cnt", "min_dura_ns", "max_dura_ns", "spread_ns"]))

# Convert to ms for readability
print("\n=== SAME IN MILLISECONDS ===")
top_spread_ms = top_spread.with_columns([
    (pl.col("min_dura_ns") / 1e6).alias("min_ms"),
    (pl.col("max_dura_ns") / 1e6).alias("max_ms"),
    (pl.col("spread_ns") / 1e6).alias("spread_ms"),
])
print(top_spread_ms.select(["ts", "swid", "probe_name", "min_ms", "max_ms", "spread_ms"]))

# Stats on spread distribution
print("\n=== SPREAD DISTRIBUTION (ms) ===")
spread_ms = df["spread_ns"] / 1e6
print(f"Mean: {spread_ms.mean():.2f} ms")
print(f"Median: {spread_ms.median():.2f} ms")
print(f"P99: {spread_ms.quantile(0.99):.2f} ms")
print(f"Max: {spread_ms.max():.2f} ms")
EOF

content.thinking:
Very clear pattern:
- There are clear anomalous `MPI_Allreduce` calls with spreads of 50-95ms (while median is 0.36ms)
- The straggler rank(s) have min_dura ~0.03ms (30μs) while others wait up to 95ms
- This happens at random timesteps (90, 101, 112, 133, 145, 166, 210, 232, 244, 254...)

Now I need to find which ranks are the stragglers - the ones with the lowest dura_ns at these anomalous swids.

content.text: Clear anomalies found - `MPI_Allreduce` calls with 50-95ms spreads (median is only 0.36ms). Let me find the straggler ranks.

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

# Load raw collectives
print("=== LOADING RAW COLLECTIVES ===")
df = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_raw/**/*.parquet").collect()
print(f"Total rows: {len(df)}")

# Focus on the anomalous swids (the ones with high spread)
anomalous_swids = [1273, 1341, 1393, 1153, 801, 561, 501, 733, 621, 913]

# For each anomalous swid, find the straggler ranks (lowest dura_ns)
print("\n=== STRAGGLER RANKS FOR ANOMALOUS SWIDS ===")
for swid in anomalous_swids[:5]:  # Top 5
    subset = df.filter((pl.col("swid") == swid) & (pl.col("probe_name") == "MPI_Allreduce"))
    if len(subset) > 0:
        # Find ranks with lowest dura_ns (stragglers)
        stragglers = subset.sort("dura_ns").head(10)
        ts = stragglers["timestep"][0]
        print(f"\nswid={swid} (ts={ts}):")
        print(f"  Straggler ranks (lowest dura): {stragglers['rank'].to_list()}")
        print(f"  Their dura_ns: {[f'{d/1e6:.3f}ms' for d in stragglers['dura_ns'].to_list()]}")

# Aggregate: which ranks are most frequently stragglers?
print("\n=== RANK FREQUENCY AS STRAGGLER (across all anomalous swids) ===")
straggler_counts = {}
for swid in anomalous_swids:
    subset = df.filter((pl.col("swid") == swid) & (pl.col("probe_name") == "MPI_Allreduce"))
    if len(subset) > 0:
        stragglers = subset.sort("dura_ns").head(5)["rank"].to_list()
        for r in stragglers:
            straggler_counts[r] = straggler_counts.get(r, 0) + 1

# Sort by frequency
sorted_stragglers = sorted(straggler_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])
print("Top 20 most frequent straggler ranks:")
for rank, cnt in sorted_stragglers[:20]:
    print(f"  Rank {rank}: {cnt} times")
EOF

import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

# Load kokkos events for analysis
print("=== LOADING KOKKOS EVENTS ===")
kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_events/**/*.parquet").collect()
print(f"Total kokkos events: {len(kokkos)}")
print(f"Columns: {kokkos.columns}")

# Look at one anomalous case: ts=232, swid=1273, straggler rank=2383
ts = 232
swid = 1273
straggler_rank = 2383

print(f"\n=== KOKKOS EVENTS FOR TS={ts}, STRAGGLER RANK={straggler_rank} ===")
straggler_events = kokkos.filter((pl.col("timestep") == ts) & (pl.col("rank") == straggler_rank))
print(f"Total events for straggler: {len(straggler_events)}")

# Show events sorted by duration
print("\nTop 20 events by duration:")
top_dur = straggler_events.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(20)
print(top_dur.select(["probe_name", "swid", "dura_ns"]).with_columns((pl.col("dura_ns")/1e6).alias("dura_ms")))

# Compare with a non-straggler rank in same timestep
normal_rank = 0
print(f"\n=== COMPARISON: NORMAL RANK={normal_rank} SAME TIMESTEP ===")
normal_events = kokkos.filter((pl.col("timestep") == ts) & (pl.col("rank") == normal_rank))
print(f"Total events for normal rank: {len(normal_events)}")
print("\nTop 20 events by duration:")
top_dur_normal = normal_events.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(20)
print(top_dur_normal.select(["probe_name", "swid", "dura_ns"]).with_columns((pl.col("dura_ns")/1e6).alias("dura_ms")))
EOF

input.content:
nodes:
  # Raw mpi_collectives - always collect
  mpi_coll_raw:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM mpi_collectives

  # Kokkos events taking >1ms (filter out fast ones to reduce volume)
  kokkos_slow:
    from: [kokkos_events]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM kokkos_events
      WHERE dura_ns > 1000000

  # Per-swid stats for quick anomaly detection
  mpi_coll_swid_stats:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        timestep as ts,
        swid,
        probe_name,
        COUNT(*) as cnt,
        MIN(dura_ns) as min_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) as max_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) - MIN(dura_ns) as spread_ns
      FROM mpi_collectives
      GROUP BY timestep, swid, probe_name

sinks:
  mpi_coll_raw_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_raw
    type: parquet
  kokkos_slow_pq:
    from: kokkos_slow
    type: parquet
  mpi_coll_swid_stats_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_swid_stats
    type: parquet

import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

# Load slow kokkos events
print("=== LOADING SLOW KOKKOS EVENTS (>1ms) ===")
kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()
print(f"Total slow kokkos events: {len(kokkos)}")
if len(kokkos) > 0:
    print(f"Timestep range: {kokkos['timestep'].min()} - {kokkos['timestep'].max()}")
    
    # Top probes by max duration
    print("\n=== TOP PROBES BY MAX DURATION ===")
    top_probes = kokkos.group_by("probe_name").agg([
        pl.col("dura_ns").max().alias("max_dura_ns"),
        pl.col("dura_ns").mean().alias("avg_dura_ns"),
        pl.len().alias("cnt")
    ]).sort("max_dura_ns", descending=True).head(15)
    print(top_probes.with_columns([
        (pl.col("max_dura_ns")/1e6).alias("max_ms"),
        (pl.col("avg_dura_ns")/1e6).alias("avg_ms")
    ]).select(["probe_name", "max_ms", "avg_ms", "cnt"]))
    
    # Look at the slowest individual events
    print("\n=== SLOWEST INDIVIDUAL EVENTS ===")
    slowest = kokkos.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(20)
    print(slowest.select(["timestep", "swid", "rank", "probe_name", "dura_ns"]).with_columns(
        (pl.col("dura_ns")/1e6).alias("dura_ms")
    ))
else:
    print("No slow kokkos events captured!")
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

# Load data
coll = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_raw/**/*.parquet").collect()
kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()
swid_stats = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_swid_stats/**/*.parquet").collect()

# Filter to ts >= 278 (new run period)
coll = coll.filter(pl.col("timestep") >= 278)
swid_stats = swid_stats.filter(pl.col("ts") >= 278)

# Find anomalous swids with high spread
print("=== ANOMALOUS SWIDS (ts >= 278) ===")
anomalous = swid_stats.filter(pl.col("spread_ns") > 50_000_000).sort("spread_ns", descending=True)  # >50ms spread
print(anomalous.head(10).select(["ts", "swid", "probe_name", "spread_ns"]).with_columns(
    (pl.col("spread_ns")/1e6).alias("spread_ms")
))

# For each anomalous swid, find straggler rank and look at their kokkos events
print("\n=== CORRELATING STRAGGLERS WITH KOKKOS EVENTS ===")
for row in anomalous.head(5).iter_rows(named=True):
    ts = row["ts"]
    swid = row["swid"]
    probe = row["probe_name"]
    
    # Find straggler rank (lowest dura_ns in this collective)
    subset = coll.filter((pl.col("swid") == swid) & (pl.col("probe_name") == probe))
    straggler = subset.sort("dura_ns").head(1)
    if len(straggler) > 0:
        straggler_rank = straggler["rank"][0]
        straggler_dura = straggler["dura_ns"][0] / 1e6
        
        print(f"\nts={ts}, swid={swid}, straggler_rank={straggler_rank} (dura={straggler_dura:.3f}ms)")
        
        # Find kokkos events for this rank in the same or previous swid
        # Events BEFORE the collective would have swid < current swid (or in same timestep)
        rank_kokkos = kokkos.filter(
            (pl.col("timestep") == ts) & 
            (pl.col("rank") == straggler_rank)
        ).sort("dura_ns", descending=True)
        
        if len(rank_kokkos) > 0:
            print(f"  Top kokkos events for this rank in ts={ts}:")
            for k in rank_kokkos.head(5).iter_rows(named=True):
                print(f"    swid={k['swid']}, {k['probe_name']}: {k['dura_ns']/1e6:.2f}ms")
        else:
            print(f"  No slow kokkos events for rank {straggler_rank} in ts={ts}")
EOF

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

coll = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_raw/**/*.parquet").collect()
kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()

# Focus on one anomalous case: ts=376, swid=2061
ts = 376
swid = 2061

# Get all ranks' collective durations for this swid
coll_subset = coll.filter((pl.col("swid") == swid) & (pl.col("probe_name") == "MPI_Allreduce"))
coll_subset = coll_subset.with_columns((pl.col("dura_ns")/1e6).alias("coll_dura_ms"))

# Get straggler (lowest dura) and non-straggler (highest dura) ranks
stragglers = coll_subset.sort("dura_ns").head(10)["rank"].to_list()
non_stragglers = coll_subset.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(10)["rank"].to_list()

print(f"=== ANALYSIS FOR ts={ts}, swid={swid} ===")
print(f"Straggler ranks (arrived late): {stragglers}")
print(f"Non-straggler ranks (waited long): {non_stragglers}")

# Compare kokkos events between stragglers and non-stragglers
kokkos_ts = kokkos.filter(pl.col("timestep") == ts)

print("\n=== MESH INITIALIZE DURATIONS ===")
mesh_init = kokkos_ts.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize")

straggler_init = mesh_init.filter(pl.col("rank").is_in(stragglers))
nonstraggler_init = mesh_init.filter(pl.col("rank").is_in(non_stragglers))

print(f"Straggler ranks Mesh::Initialize avg: {straggler_init['dura_ns'].mean()/1e6:.2f}ms")
print(f"Non-straggler ranks Mesh::Initialize avg: {nonstraggler_init['dura_ns'].mean()/1e6:.2f}ms")

print("\n=== BUILDANDPOST::CLEAR DURATIONS ===")
build_clear = kokkos_ts.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear")

if len(build_clear) > 0:
    straggler_clear = build_clear.filter(pl.col("rank").is_in(stragglers))
    nonstraggler_clear = build_clear.filter(pl.col("rank").is_in(non_stragglers))
    
    print(f"Straggler ranks have BuildAndPost::Clear: {len(straggler_clear)}")
    print(f"Non-straggler ranks have BuildAndPost::Clear: {len(nonstraggler_clear)}")
    
    if len(straggler_clear) > 0:
        print(f"Straggler BuildAndPost::Clear avg: {straggler_clear['dura_ns'].mean()/1e6:.2f}ms")
    if len(nonstraggler_clear) > 0:
        print(f"Non-straggler BuildAndPost::Clear avg: {nonstraggler_clear['dura_ns'].mean()/1e6:.2f}ms")
    
    # Show individual values
    print("\n=== ALL BUILDANDPOST::CLEAR EVENTS (sample) ===")
    all_clear = build_clear.with_columns([
        (pl.col("dura_ns")/1e6).alias("dura_ms"),
        pl.col("rank").is_in(stragglers).alias("is_straggler")
    ]).sort("dura_ns", descending=True)
    print(all_clear.head(20).select(["rank", "swid", "dura_ms", "is_straggler"]))
else:
    print("No BuildAndPost::Clear events found in this timestep")
EOF

import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()

# Find the ranks with the longest redistribution times
print("=== REDISTRIBUTION DURATIONS BY RANK ===")
rank_redist = redist.group_by("rank").agg([
    pl.col("dura_ns").max().alias("max_dura_ns"),
    pl.col("dura_ns").mean().alias("avg_dura_ns"),
    pl.len().alias("cnt")
]).sort("max_dura_ns", descending=True)

print("Top 20 ranks by max redistribution duration:")
print(rank_redist.head(20).with_columns([
    (pl.col("max_dura_ns")/1e6).alias("max_ms"),
    (pl.col("avg_dura_ns")/1e6).alias("avg_ms")
]).select(["rank", "max_ms", "avg_ms", "cnt"]))

# What's the distribution across all ranks?
print("\n=== REDISTRIBUTION DURATION DISTRIBUTION ===")
all_durations = rank_redist["max_dura_ns"] / 1e6
print(f"Total ranks with redistribution events: {len(rank_redist)}")
print(f"Min: {all_durations.min():.2f}ms")
print(f"Median: {all_durations.median():.2f}ms")
print(f"P90: {all_durations.quantile(0.90):.2f}ms")
print(f"P99: {all_durations.quantile(0.99):.2f}ms")
print(f"Max: {all_durations.max():.2f}ms")

# Look at Step 9 (Recv data and unpack) - receiving mesh blocks

    print("\n=== STEP 9: RECV DATA AND UNPACK (receiving mesh blocks) ===")
    rank_step9 = step9.group_by("rank").agg([
        pl.col("dura_ns").max().alias("max_dura_ns"),
        pl.len().alias("cnt")
    ]).sort("max_dura_ns", descending=True)
    print(rank_step9.head(20).with_columns((pl.col("max_dura_ns")/1e6).alias("max_ms")))
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()

# Get all ranks that have BuildAndPost::Clear events
clear_events = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear")
ranks_with_clear = set(clear_events["rank"].to_list())

# Get redistribution durations for all ranks
redist = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::RedistributeAndRefineMeshBlocks")

# Compare mean redistribution duration for ranks with/without Clear
print("=== REDISTRIBUTION DURATION: RANKS WITH vs WITHOUT CLEAR ===")

with_clear = redist.filter(pl.col("rank").is_in(ranks_with_clear))
without_clear = redist.filter(~pl.col("rank").is_in(ranks_with_clear))

print(f"Ranks with Clear: {len(ranks_with_clear)}")
print(f"  Mean redistribution: {with_clear['dura_ns'].mean()/1e6:.2f}ms")
print(f"  Max redistribution: {with_clear['dura_ns'].max()/1e6:.2f}ms")

print(f"\nRanks without Clear: {4096 - len(ranks_with_clear)}")
print(f"  Mean redistribution: {without_clear['dura_ns'].mean()/1e6:.2f}ms")
print(f"  Max redistribution: {without_clear['dura_ns'].max()/1e6:.2f}ms")

# Now look at what triggers Clear - check if it correlates with receiving more blocks
print("\n=== CHECKING STEP 7a/7b (PACK/SEND) vs STEP 9 (RECV) BALANCE ===")
step7a = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Step 7a: Pack buffers")
step9 = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Step 9: Recv data and unpack")

# Ranks that have Step 9 but maybe less Step 7a activity
for rank in list(ranks_with_clear)[:5]:
    r_step7a = step7a.filter(pl.col("rank") == rank)
    r_step9 = step9.filter(pl.col("rank") == rank)
    print(f"\nRank {rank} (has Clear):")
    print(f"  Step 7a (pack/send) events: {len(r_step7a)}")
    print(f"  Step 9 (recv/unpack) events: {len(r_step9)}")
    if len(r_step9) > 0:
        print(f"  Max Step 9 duration: {r_step9['dura_ns'].max()/1e6:.2f}ms")

# Compare with ranks without Clear
print("\n--- Ranks without Clear (sample) ---")
sample_without = [r for r in [0, 100, 500, 1000, 2000] if r not in ranks_with_clear]
for rank in sample_without[:5]:
    r_step7a = step7a.filter(pl.col("rank") == rank)
    r_step9 = step9.filter(pl.col("rank") == rank)
    print(f"\nRank {rank} (no Clear):")
    print(f"  Step 7a (pack/send) events: {len(r_step7a)}")
    print(f"  Step 9 (recv/unpack) events: {len(r_step9)}")
    if len(r_step9) > 0:
        print(f"  Max Step 9 duration: {r_step9['dura_ns'].max()/1e6:.2f}ms")
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()
coll = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_raw/**/*.parquet").collect()

# Get Clear events with durations
clear_events = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear")
print("=== BUILDANDPOST::CLEAR DURATION DISTRIBUTION ===")
print(f"Total Clear events: {len(clear_events)}")
clear_ms = clear_events["dura_ns"] / 1e6
print(f"Min: {clear_ms.min():.2f}ms")
print(f"Median: {clear_ms.median():.2f}ms")
print(f"P95: {clear_ms.quantile(0.95):.2f}ms")
print(f"Max: {clear_ms.max():.2f}ms")

# Show the actual Clear events
print("\n=== SAMPLE CLEAR EVENTS ===")
print(clear_events.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(20).with_columns([
    (pl.col("dura_ns")/1e6).alias("dura_ms")
]).select(["timestep", "swid", "rank", "dura_ms"]))

# Now correlate with collective stragglers
print("\n=== CORRELATION: CLEAR EVENTS -> COLLECTIVE STRAGGLERS ===")

# For each Clear event, check if the same rank is the straggler in a subsequent collective
matches = 0
total = 0
for row in clear_events.iter_rows(named=True):
    ts = row["timestep"]
    rank = row["rank"]
    clear_swid = row["swid"]
    
    # Find the next MPI_Allreduce after this swid
    next_coll = coll.filter(
        (pl.col("timestep") == ts) & 
        (pl.col("swid") > clear_swid) & 
        (pl.col("probe_name") == "MPI_Allreduce")
    ).sort("swid").head(1)
    
    if len(next_coll) > 0:
        total += 1
        coll_swid = next_coll["swid"][0]
        
        # Check if this rank is the straggler (lowest dura) in that collective
        all_ranks_coll = coll.filter(
            (pl.col("timestep") == ts) & 
            (pl.col("swid") == coll_swid) & 
            (pl.col("probe_name") == "MPI_Allreduce")
        )
        straggler_rank = all_ranks_coll.sort("dura_ns").head(1)["rank"][0]
        
        if straggler_rank == rank:
            matches += 1

print(f"Clear events followed by collective: {total}")
print(f"Clear rank is the straggler: {matches} ({100*matches/total:.1f}%)")
EOF

import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()
coll = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_raw/**/*.parquet").collect()
swid_stats = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_swid_stats/**/*.parquet").collect()

# Get Clear events with their swids
clear_events = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear")

print("=== CLEAR EVENT SWIDS ===")
print(clear_events.select(["timestep", "swid", "rank", "dura_ns"]).sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(10))

# Get the anomalous swids (high spread)
print("\n=== ANOMALOUS COLLECTIVE SWIDS ===")
anomalous = swid_stats.filter((pl.col("spread_ns") > 50_000_000) & (pl.col("ts") >= 278))
print(anomalous.sort("spread_ns", descending=True).head(10).select(["ts", "swid", "probe_name", "spread_ns"]))

# Check if Clear swids match anomalous collective swids
clear_swids = set(zip(clear_events["timestep"].to_list(), clear_events["swid"].to_list()))
anom_swids = set(zip(anomalous["ts"].to_list(), anomalous["swid"].to_list()))

print(f"\n=== MATCHING SWIDS ===")
print(f"Clear (ts, swid) pairs: {len(clear_swids)}")
print(f"Anomalous (ts, swid) pairs: {len(anom_swids)}")
print(f"Intersection: {len(clear_swids & anom_swids)}")

# The Clear events are AT the same swid as the collective, meaning the Clear 
# happens right before the collective within the same sync window
# Let me check if Clear ranks are stragglers at that swid
print("\n=== CHECKING IF CLEAR RANKS ARE STRAGGLERS ===")

sample = clear_events.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(10)
for row in sample.iter_rows(named=True):
    ts = row["timestep"]
    swid = row["swid"]
    rank = row["rank"]
    clear_dura = row["dura_ns"] / 1e6
    
    # Find collective at same swid
    coll_at_swid = coll.filter(
        (pl.col("timestep") == ts) & 
        (pl.col("swid") == swid) & 
        (pl.col("probe_name") == "MPI_Allreduce")
    )
    
    if len(coll_at_swid) > 0:
        straggler = coll_at_swid.sort("dura_ns").head(1)
        straggler_rank = straggler["rank"][0]
        straggler_dura = straggler["dura_ns"][0] / 1e6
        this_rank_coll = coll_at_swid.filter(pl.col("rank") == rank)
        this_rank_dura = this_rank_coll["dura_ns"][0] / 1e6 if len(this_rank_coll) > 0 else -1
        is_straggler = "YES" if straggler_rank == rank else "NO"
        print(f"ts={ts}, swid={swid}, rank={rank}: Clear={clear_dura:.1f}ms, Coll={this_rank_dura:.3f}ms, Straggler={is_straggler}")
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()
coll = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_raw/**/*.parquet").collect()
swid_stats = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_coll_swid_stats/**/*.parquet").collect()

# Get Clear events indexed by (ts, swid, rank)
clear_events = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear")
clear_index = set(zip(
    clear_events["timestep"].to_list(), 
    clear_events["swid"].to_list(),
    clear_events["rank"].to_list()
))

# Get anomalous swids
anomalous = swid_stats.filter((pl.col("spread_ns") > 50_000_000) & (pl.col("ts") >= 278))

print("=== STRAGGLER ANALYSIS FOR ANOMALOUS COLLECTIVES ===")
print("Checking if the straggler rank has a Clear event at the same (ts, swid)...\n")

straggler_has_clear = 0
total_anomalous = 0

for row in anomalous.iter_rows(named=True):
    ts = row["ts"]
    swid = row["swid"]
    spread_ms = row["spread_ns"] / 1e6
    
    # Find the straggler (lowest dura_ns)
    coll_at_swid = coll.filter(
        (pl.col("timestep") == ts) & 
        (pl.col("swid") == swid) & 
        (pl.col("probe_name") == "MPI_Allreduce")
    )
    
    if len(coll_at_swid) > 0:
        straggler = coll_at_swid.sort("dura_ns").head(1)
        straggler_rank = straggler["rank"][0]
        straggler_dura = straggler["dura_ns"][0] / 1e6
        
        total_anomalous += 1
        
        # Check if straggler has Clear at this (ts, swid)
        has_clear = (ts, swid, straggler_rank) in clear_index
        if has_clear:
            straggler_has_clear += 1
            
        # Also check if straggler has Clear at previous swid
        has_clear_prev = (ts, swid-1, straggler_rank) in clear_index
        
        # Get Clear duration for this rank if exists
        clear_dur = clear_events.filter(
            (pl.col("timestep") == ts) & 
            (pl.col("rank") == straggler_rank)
        )
        clear_ms = clear_dur["dura_ns"].max()/1e6 if len(clear_dur) > 0 else 0
        
        mark = "✓" if has_clear or has_clear_prev else ""
        print(f"ts={ts}, swid={swid}, spread={spread_ms:.1f}ms: straggler=rank {straggler_rank} (dura={straggler_dura:.3f}ms), Clear={clear_ms:.1f}ms {mark}")

print(f"\n=== SUMMARY ===")
print(f"Anomalous collectives: {total_anomalous}")
print(f"Straggler has Clear at same swid: {straggler_has_clear} ({100*straggler_has_clear/total_anomalous:.1f}%)")
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()

# Get Clear events grouped by timestep
clear_events = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear")

print("=== CLEAR EVENTS BY TIMESTEP ===")
by_ts = clear_events.group_by("timestep").agg([
    pl.len().alias("n_clear_ranks"),
    pl.col("dura_ns").max().alias("max_clear_ns"),
    pl.col("rank").alias("ranks")
]).sort("timestep")

for row in by_ts.head(10).iter_rows(named=True):
    ts = row["timestep"]
    n = row["n_clear_ranks"]
    max_ms = row["max_clear_ns"] / 1e6
    ranks = row["ranks"][:5]  # First 5
    print(f"ts={ts}: {n} ranks with Clear (max={max_ms:.1f}ms), sample ranks: {ranks}")

# Check Mesh::Initialize durations for Clear vs non-Clear ranks
print("\n=== MESH::INITIALIZE DURATION COMPARISON ===")
mesh_init = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize")

clear_ranks = set(clear_events["rank"].to_list())

# For a specific timestep, compare
ts = 376
ts_mesh = mesh_init.filter(pl.col("timestep") == ts)
ts_clear = ts_mesh.filter(pl.col("rank").is_in(clear_ranks))
ts_noclear = ts_mesh.filter(~pl.col("rank").is_in(clear_ranks))

print(f"\nTimestep {ts}:")
print(f"  Ranks with Clear - Mesh::Initialize avg: {ts_clear['dura_ns'].mean()/1e6:.2f}ms (n={len(ts_clear)})")
print(f"  Ranks without Clear - Mesh::Initialize avg: {ts_noclear['dura_ns'].mean()/1e6:.2f}ms (n={len(ts_noclear)})")

# What about variance in Mesh::Initialize overall?
print("\n=== MESH::INITIALIZE DURATION VARIANCE ===")
mi_stats = mesh_init.group_by("timestep").agg([
    pl.col("dura_ns").min().alias("min_ns"),
    pl.col("dura_ns").max().alias("max_ns"),
    pl.col("dura_ns").mean().alias("mean_ns"),
    (pl.col("dura_ns").max() - pl.col("dura_ns").min()).alias("spread_ns")
]).sort("spread_ns", descending=True)

print(mi_stats.head(10).with_columns([
    (pl.col("min_ns")/1e6).alias("min_ms"),
    (pl.col("max_ns")/1e6).alias("max_ms"),
    (pl.col("spread_ns")/1e6).alias("spread_ms")
]).select(["timestep", "min_ms", "max_ms", "spread_ms"]))
EOF

Now I have a very complete picture. Let me summarize the findings:

1. **The anomaly pattern**: 
   - Every ~10-11 timesteps, AMR (Adaptive Mesh Refinement) runs to rebalance the mesh
   - During AMR, mesh blocks are redistributed between ranks
   - Some ranks (1-11 per timestep, <0.3% of 4096) need to clear their communication buffers (`ClearCommBuffers_bad`)
   - These Clear operations take 70-84ms
   - The overall `Mesh::Initialize` varies from ~11ms to ~105ms (spread of ~90ms)
   - This causes stragglers - one or a few ranks are still busy while 4000+ ranks wait at `MPI_Allreduce`

2. **Root cause**:
   - The `ClearCommBuffers_bad` function clears `boundary_comm_map` and `boundary_comm_flxcor_map`
   - These are `unordered_map<tuple, CommBuffer>` data structures
   - Clearing them is expensive when they contain many entries (deallocating buffers)
   - The "_bad" suffix in the function name suggests this is a known issue

3. **Why it's random**:
   - The ranks that need to clear depend on mesh redistribution decisions
   - This depends on where work imbalance exists and how the load balancer decides to move blocks
   - As the simulation evolves, different regions become hot/cold, so different ranks get affected each time

4. **Key metrics**:
   - MPI_Allreduce spread: 50-115ms (anomalous timesteps)
   - BuildAndPost::Clear: 70-84ms
   - Mesh::Initialize spread: 88-94ms between min/max rank
   - ~114 unique ranks affected across the 187 timesteps analyzed

input.content:
# AMR Performance Anomaly Analysis

## Summary

The performance anomaly is caused by **uneven mesh rebalancing overhead during AMR (Adaptive Mesh Refinement)**. During periodic load balancing, a small subset of ranks (1-11 per timestep, <0.3% of 4096) must clear and rebuild their communication buffer maps, which takes 70-84ms. This creates stragglers that delay MPI collectives while 4000+ other ranks wait.

## Evidence

### 1. Collective Duration Spread

Anomalous `MPI_Allreduce` calls show 50-115ms spread between fastest and slowest ranks:
- Median spread: 0.36ms (normal)
- Anomalous spreads: 50-115ms
- Straggler ranks have ~30μs collective duration (arrived late)
- Other ranks wait 80-95ms

### 2. Straggler Pattern

Stragglers are **random** across timesteps:
- Different ranks affected each time
- No single rank appears more than once in top stragglers
- Pattern follows AMR rebalancing decisions

### 3. Root Cause Chain

For each anomalous collective, the straggler rank is executing:

```
region::LoadBalancingAndAdaptiveMeshRefinement  (65-124ms)
  └── region::RedistributeAndRefineMeshBlocks   (42-114ms)
        └── region::Mesh::Initialize             (34-102ms)
              └── region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear  (70-84ms)  ← BOTTLENECK
```

### 4. The Bottleneck: ClearCommBuffers_bad

In `mesh.cpp:1245-1248`:
```cpp
void Mesh::ClearCommBuffers_bad(int num_partitions) {
    boundary_comm_map.clear();
    boundary_comm_flxcor_map.clear();
}
```

This clears an `unordered_map<channel_key_t, CommBuffer>` which:
- Contains communication buffers for boundary exchanges
- Has expensive destructors that deallocate memory
- Takes 70-84ms when the rank has many entries

The "_bad" suffix suggests this is a **known performance issue** in Parthenon.

### 5. Why Only Some Ranks

- Only 114 unique ranks (out of 4096) had `Clear` events across 187 timesteps
- These ranks are receiving **new mesh blocks** during redistribution
- They must clear old communication state before setting up new channels
- Ranks that keep their blocks skip this step

### 6. Mesh::Initialize Variance

```
Timestep 376:
  Ranks WITH Clear:    37.23ms avg  (n=114)
  Ranks WITHOUT Clear: 34.68ms avg  (n=3982)

Mesh::Initialize spread: min=10.7ms, max=104.8ms
```

The ~90ms spread directly maps to the collective straggling.

## Timeline of AMR Anomaly

1. **T+0**: LoadBalancingAndAdaptiveMeshRefinement starts (all ranks)
2. **T+10ms**: Load balancer decides which blocks to move
3. **T+20ms**: Blocks are packed and sent (Step 7)
4. **T+30ms**: Most ranks receive and unpack (Step 9) - fast path
5. **T+30-110ms**: A few ranks clear old buffers (`ClearCommBuffers_bad`) - **slow path**
6. **T+30ms**: Fast ranks hit MPI_Allreduce, start waiting
7. **T+110ms**: Slow ranks finish Clear, hit MPI_Allreduce
8. **T+110ms**: Collective completes with 80ms+ spread

## Why It Appears Random

The straggler ranks depend on:
1. Current mesh block distribution
2. Physics driving refinement (where to add/remove resolution)
3. Load balancer's cost function decisions
4. Previous timestep's block assignments

As the simulation evolves, different spatial regions become computationally expensive, leading to different redistribution patterns.

## Flows Used

1. `0-probe-counts.yaml` - Initial probe discovery
2. `1-baseline-collectives.yaml` - MPI collective tracking
3. `2-straggler-hunt.yaml` - Kokkos events >1ms + collective raw data

## Recommendations

1. **Optimize ClearCommBuffers_bad**: The function name suggests awareness of the issue. Consider:
   - Lazy clearing (only clear when needed)
   - Amortizing cleanup across timesteps
   - Using a different data structure with cheaper destruction

2. **Load balancer tuning**: Balance the "mesh block count" vs "communication buffer setup cost"

3. **Overlap Clear with communication**: The Clear could potentially happen while waiting for new blocks to arrive

## Key Metrics

toolUseResult.content:
# AMR Performance Anomaly Analysis

## Summary

The performance anomaly is caused by **uneven mesh rebalancing overhead during AMR (Adaptive Mesh Refinement)**. During periodic load balancing, a small subset of ranks (1-11 per timestep, <0.3% of 4096) must clear and rebuild their communication buffer maps, which takes 70-84ms. This creates stragglers that delay MPI collectives while 4000+ other ranks wait.

## Evidence

### 1. Collective Duration Spread

Anomalous `MPI_Allreduce` calls show 50-115ms spread between fastest and slowest ranks:
- Median spread: 0.36ms (normal)
- Anomalous spreads: 50-115ms
- Straggler ranks have ~30μs collective duration (arrived late)
- Other ranks wait 80-95ms

### 2. Straggler Pattern

Stragglers are **random** across timesteps:
- Different ranks affected each time
- No single rank appears more than once in top stragglers
- Pattern follows AMR rebalancing decisions

### 3. Root Cause Chain

For each anomalous collective, the straggler rank is executing:

```
region::LoadBalancingAndAdaptiveMeshRefinement  (65-124ms)
  └── region::RedistributeAndRefineMeshBlocks   (42-114ms)
        └── region::Mesh::Initialize             (34-102ms)
              └── region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear  (70-84ms)  ← BOTTLENECK
```

### 4. The Bottleneck: ClearCommBuffers_bad

In `mesh.cpp:1245-1248`:
```cpp
void Mesh::ClearCommBuffers_bad(int num_partitions) {
    boundary_comm_map.clear();
    boundary_comm_flxcor_map.clear();
}
```

This clears an `unordered_map<channel_key_t, CommBuffer>` which:
- Contains communication buffers for boundary exchanges
- Has expensive destructors that deallocate memory
- Takes 70-84ms when the rank has many entries

The "_bad" suffix suggests this is a **known performance issue** in Parthenon.

### 5. Why Only Some Ranks

- Only 114 unique ranks (out of 4096) had `Clear` events across 187 timesteps
- These ranks are receiving **new mesh blocks** during redistribution
- They must clear old communication state before setting up new channels
- Ranks that keep their blocks skip this step

### 6. Mesh::Initialize Variance

```
Timestep 376:
  Ranks WITH Clear:    37.23ms avg  (n=114)
  Ranks WITHOUT Clear: 34.68ms avg  (n=3982)

Mesh::Initialize spread: min=10.7ms, max=104.8ms
```

The ~90ms spread directly maps to the collective straggling.

## Timeline of AMR Anomaly

1. **T+0**: LoadBalancingAndAdaptiveMeshRefinement starts (all ranks)
2. **T+10ms**: Load balancer decides which blocks to move
3. **T+20ms**: Blocks are packed and sent (Step 7)
4. **T+30ms**: Most ranks receive and unpack (Step 9) - fast path
5. **T+30-110ms**: A few ranks clear old buffers (`ClearCommBuffers_bad`) - **slow path**
6. **T+30ms**: Fast ranks hit MPI_Allreduce, start waiting
7. **T+110ms**: Slow ranks finish Clear, hit MPI_Allreduce
8. **T+110ms**: Collective completes with 80ms+ spread

## Why It Appears Random

The straggler ranks depend on:
1. Current mesh block distribution
2. Physics driving refinement (where to add/remove resolution)
3. Load balancer's cost function decisions
4. Previous timestep's block assignments

As the simulation evolves, different spatial regions become computationally expensive, leading to different redistribution patterns.

## Flows Used

1. `0-probe-counts.yaml` - Initial probe discovery
2. `1-baseline-collectives.yaml` - MPI collective tracking
3. `2-straggler-hunt.yaml` - Kokkos events >1ms + collective raw data

## Recommendations

1. **Optimize ClearCommBuffers_bad**: The function name suggests awareness of the issue. Consider:
   - Lazy clearing (only clear when needed)
   - Amortizing cleanup across timesteps
   - Using a different data structure with cheaper destruction

2. **Load balancer tuning**: Balance the "mesh block count" vs "communication buffer setup cost"

3. **Overlap Clear with communication**: The Clear could potentially happen while waiting for new blocks to arrive

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total ranks | 4096 |
| Ranks with Clear per timestep | 1-11 (0.0-0.3%) |
| Clear duration | 70-84ms |
| Collective spread (anomalous) | 50-115ms |
| Collective spread (normal) | 0.36ms median |
| AMR frequency | ~every 10-11 timesteps |

input.content:
# AMR Performance Anomaly Analysis

## Summary

The performance anomaly is caused by **blocking MPI operations hidden inside CommBuffer destructors** during mesh rebalancing. When `boundary_comm_map.clear()` is called, each `CommBuffer` destructor calls `MPI_Wait()` or `MPI_Cancel() + MPI_Wait()` to ensure pending communication completes. With ~200 buffers per rank, this creates serialized blocking waits totaling 70-84ms for affected ranks.

## The Real Root Cause

### CommBuffer Destructor (communication_buffer.cpp:45-74)

```cpp
template <class T>
CommBuffer<T>::~CommBuffer() {
#ifdef MPI_PARALLEL
  if (my_request_.use_count() == 1) {
    int flag;
    MPI_Status status;
    PARTHENON_MPI_CHECK(MPI_Test(my_request_.get(), &flag, &status));

    if (!flag) {  // Request not complete
      if (*comm_type_ == BuffCommType::sender) {
        PARTHENON_MPI_CHECK(MPI_Wait(my_request_.get(), MPI_STATUS_IGNORE));  // BLOCKS!
      } else {
        PARTHENON_MPI_CHECK(MPI_Cancel(my_request_.get()));  // Cancel receive
        PARTHENON_MPI_CHECK(MPI_Wait(my_request_.get(), MPI_STATUS_IGNORE));  // BLOCKS!
      }
    }
  }
#endif
}
```

### Why `boundary_comm_map.clear()` is Expensive

1. Each rank has ~200 communication buffers (boundary exchange channels with neighbors)
2. During AMR redistribution, mesh blocks move between ranks
3. Ranks receiving new blocks must clear their old communication state
4. `clear()` calls destructor on each `CommBuffer`
5. Each destructor potentially calls `MPI_Wait()` - **blocking**
6. Serialized blocking waits accumulate to 70-84ms

### Evidence: MPI Message Volume

```
Per timestep:
- MPI_Irecv: ~765,000 calls
- MPI_Isend: ~765,000 calls
- Per rank average: ~187 communication channels
```

## Why It Appears Random

1. AMR runs every ~10 timesteps to rebalance computational load
2. The load balancer decides which mesh blocks to move based on work imbalance
3. Ranks that **receive new blocks** must clear old buffers (trigger the expensive Clear)
4. As simulation physics evolve, different regions become hot/cold
5. Different ranks need block redistribution each time → different stragglers

## Call Chain to Bottleneck

```
Mesh::Initialize (mesh.cpp:1104-1123)
  └── Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost
        └── Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear
              └── ClearCommBuffers_bad(num_partitions)
                    └── boundary_comm_map.clear()
                          └── ~CommBuffer() × 200
                                └── MPI_Wait() or MPI_Cancel()+MPI_Wait()  ← BLOCKING
```

## Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total ranks | 4096 |
| Buffers per rank | ~200 |
| Ranks with Clear per timestep | 1-11 (0.0-0.3%) |
| Clear duration | 70-84ms |
| Collective spread (anomalous) | 50-115ms |
| Collective spread (normal) | 0.36ms median |
| AMR frequency | ~every 10-11 timesteps |

## The Straggler Pattern

```
Timestep N (AMR runs):
  T+0:    LoadBalancingAndAdaptiveMeshRefinement starts
  T+10ms: Load balancer decides block redistribution
  T+20ms: Blocks packed and sent (Step 7)
  T+30ms: Most ranks receive/unpack - fast path (no Clear needed)
  T+30ms: Fast ranks hit MPI_Allreduce, start waiting...

  T+30-110ms: 1-11 ranks call boundary_comm_map.clear()
              → 200 CommBuffer destructors
              → 200 potential MPI_Wait() calls
              → BLOCKING for 70-84ms

  T+110ms: Slow ranks finish Clear, hit MPI_Allreduce
  T+110ms: Collective completes (4000+ ranks waited 80ms!)
```

## Why Only Some Ranks Need Clear

Looking at the data:
- 114 unique ranks had Clear events across 187 timesteps
- These ranks are receiving **new mesh blocks** after redistribution
- They need to tear down old neighbor communication channels
- Ranks keeping their blocks skip this entirely

## Recommendations

1. **Non-blocking cleanup**: Instead of `MPI_Wait()` in destructor, queue requests for later completion or use `MPI_Request_free()`

2. **Amortize cleanup**: Clear buffers incrementally over multiple timesteps rather than all at once

3. **Async cancel**: Use `MPI_Cancel()` without blocking wait where possible

4. **Overlap with computation**: Start clearing old buffers while new blocks are still being received

5. **Buffer pooling**: Reuse communication buffers instead of destroying/recreating them

## Flows Used

1. `0-probe-counts.yaml` - Initial probe discovery
2. `1-baseline-collectives.yaml` - MPI collective tracking
3. `2-straggler-hunt.yaml` - Kokkos events >1ms + collective raw data

## Files Analyzed

- `parthenon/src/mesh/mesh.cpp:1104-1248` - Mesh initialization and ClearCommBuffers_bad
- `parthenon/src/utils/communication_buffer.cpp:45-74` - CommBuffer destructor with MPI_Wait
- `parthenon/src/utils/communication_buffer.hpp:42-126` - CommBuffer class definition

# AMR Performance Anomaly Analysis

## Summary

The performance anomaly is caused by **blocking MPI operations hidden inside CommBuffer destructors** during mesh rebalancing. When `boundary_comm_map.clear()` is called, each `CommBuffer` destructor calls `MPI_Wait()` or `MPI_Cancel() + MPI_Wait()` to ensure pending communication completes. With ~200 buffers per rank, this creates serialized blocking waits totaling 70-84ms for affected ranks.

## The Real Root Cause

### CommBuffer Destructor (communication_buffer.cpp:45-74)

```cpp
template <class T>
CommBuffer<T>::~CommBuffer() {
#ifdef MPI_PARALLEL
  if (my_request_.use_count() == 1) {
    int flag;
    MPI_Status status;
    PARTHENON_MPI_CHECK(MPI_Test(my_request_.get(), &flag, &status));

    if (!flag) {  // Request not complete
      if (*comm_type_ == BuffCommType::sender) {
        PARTHENON_MPI_CHECK(MPI_Wait(my_request_.get(), MPI_STATUS_IGNORE));  // BLOCKS!
      } else {
        PARTHENON_MPI_CHECK(MPI_Cancel(my_request_.get()));  // Cancel receive
        PARTHENON_MPI_CHECK(MPI_Wait(my_request_.get(), MPI_STATUS_IGNORE));  // BLOCKS!
      }
    }
  }
#endif
}
```

### Why `boundary_comm_map.clear()` is Expensive

1. Each rank has ~200 communication buffers (boundary exchange channels with neighbors)
2. During AMR redistribution, mesh blocks move between ranks
3. Ranks receiving new blocks must clear their old communication state
4. `clear()` calls destructor on each `CommBuffer`
5. Each destructor potentially calls `MPI_Wait()` - **blocking**
6. Serialized blocking waits accumulate to 70-84ms

### Evidence: MPI Message Volume

```
Per timestep:
- MPI_Irecv: ~765,000 calls
- MPI_Isend: ~765,000 calls
- Per rank average: ~187 communication channels
```

## Why It Appears Random

1. AMR runs every ~10 timesteps to rebalance computational load
2. The load balancer decides which mesh blocks to move based on work imbalance
3. Ranks that **receive new blocks** must clear old buffers (trigger the expensive Clear)
4. As simulation physics evolve, different regions become hot/cold
5. Different ranks need block redistribution each time → different stragglers

## Call Chain to Bottleneck

```
Mesh::Initialize (mesh.cpp:1104-1123)
  └── Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost
        └── Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear
              └── ClearCommBuffers_bad(num_partitions)
                    └── boundary_comm_map.clear()
                          └── ~CommBuffer() × 200
                                └── MPI_Wait() or MPI_Cancel()+MPI_Wait()  ← BLOCKING
```

## Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total ranks | 4096 |
| Buffers per rank | ~200 |
| Ranks with Clear per timestep | 1-11 (0.0-0.3%) |
| Clear duration | 70-84ms |
| Collective spread (anomalous) | 50-115ms |
| Collective spread (normal) | 0.36ms median |
| AMR frequency | ~every 10-11 timesteps |

## The Straggler Pattern

```
Timestep N (AMR runs):
  T+0:    LoadBalancingAndAdaptiveMeshRefinement starts
  T+10ms: Load balancer decides block redistribution
  T+20ms: Blocks packed and sent (Step 7)
  T+30ms: Most ranks receive/unpack - fast path (no Clear needed)
  T+30ms: Fast ranks hit MPI_Allreduce, start waiting...

  T+30-110ms: 1-11 ranks call boundary_comm_map.clear()
              → 200 CommBuffer destructors
              → 200 potential MPI_Wait() calls
              → BLOCKING for 70-84ms

  T+110ms: Slow ranks finish Clear, hit MPI_Allreduce
  T+110ms: Collective completes (4000+ ranks waited 80ms!)
```

## Why Only Some Ranks Need Clear

Looking at the data:
- 114 unique ranks had Clear events across 187 timesteps
- These ranks are receiving **new mesh blocks** after redistribution
- They need to tear down old neighbor communication channels
- Ranks keeping their blocks skip this entirely

## Recommendations

1. **Non-blocking cleanup**: Instead of `MPI_Wait()` in destructor, queue requests for later completion or use `MPI_Request_free()`

2. **Amortize cleanup**: Clear buffers incrementally over multiple timesteps rather than all at once

3. **Async cancel**: Use `MPI_Cancel()` without blocking wait where possible

4. **Overlap with computation**: Start clearing old buffers while new blocks are still being received

5. **Buffer pooling**: Reuse communication buffers instead of destroying/recreating them

## Flows Used

1. `0-probe-counts.yaml` - Initial probe discovery
2. `1-baseline-collectives.yaml` - MPI collective tracking
3. `2-straggler-hunt.yaml` - Kokkos events >1ms + collective raw data

## Files Analyzed

- `parthenon/src/mesh/mesh.cpp:1104-1248` - Mesh initialization and ClearCommBuffers_bad
- `parthenon/src/utils/communication_buffer.cpp:45-74` - CommBuffer destructor with MPI_Wait
- `parthenon/src/utils/communication_buffer.hpp:42-126` - CommBuffer class definition

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# AMR Performance Anomaly Analysis

## Summary

The performance anomaly is caused by **uneven mesh rebalancing overhead during AMR (Adaptive Mesh Refinement)**. During periodic load balancing, a small subset of ranks (1-11 per timestep, <0.3% of 4096) must clear and rebuild their communication buffer maps, which takes 70-84ms. This creates stragglers that delay MPI collectives while 4000+ other ranks wait.

## Evidence

### 1. Collective Duration Spread

Anomalous `MPI_Allreduce` calls show 50-115ms spread between fastest and slowest ranks:
- Median spread: 0.36ms (normal)
- Anomalous spreads: 50-115ms
- Straggler ranks have ~30μs collective duration (arrived late)
- Other ranks wait 80-95ms

### 2. Straggler Pattern

Stragglers are **random** across timesteps:
- Different ranks affected each time
- No single rank appears more than once in top stragglers
- Pattern follows AMR rebalancing decisions

### 3. Root Cause Chain

For each anomalous collective, the straggler rank is executing:

```
region::LoadBalancingAndAdaptiveMeshRefinement  (65-124ms)
  └── region::RedistributeAndRefineMeshBlocks   (42-114ms)
        └── region::Mesh::Initialize             (34-102ms)
              └── region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear  (70-84ms)  ← BOTTLENECK
```

### 4. The Bottleneck: ClearCommBuffers_bad

In `mesh.cpp:1245-1248`:
```cpp
void Mesh::ClearCommBuffers_bad(int num_partitions) {
    boundary_comm_map.clear();
    boundary_comm_flxcor_map.clear();
}
```

This clears an `unordered_map<channel_key_t, CommBuffer>` which:
- Contains communication buffers for boundary exchanges
- Has expensive destructors that deallocate memory
- Takes 70-84ms when the rank has many entries

The "_bad" suffix suggests this is a **known performance issue** in Parthenon.

### 5. Why Only Some Ranks

- Only 114 unique ranks (out of 4096) had `Clear` events across 187 timesteps
- These ranks are receiving **new mesh blocks** during redistribution
- They must clear old communication state before setting up new channels
- Ranks that keep their blocks skip this step

### 6. Mesh::Initialize Variance

```
Timestep 376:
  Ranks WITH Clear:    37.23ms avg  (n=114)
  Ranks WITHOUT Clear: 34.68ms avg  (n=3982)

Mesh::Initialize spread: min=10.7ms, max=104.8ms
```

The ~90ms spread directly maps to the collective straggling.

## Timeline of AMR Anomaly

1. **T+0**: LoadBalancingAndAdaptiveMeshRefinement starts (all ranks)
2. **T+10ms**: Load balancer decides which blocks to move
3. **T+20ms**: Blocks are packed and sent (Step 7)
4. **T+30ms**: Most ranks receive and unpack (Step 9) - fast path
5. **T+30-110ms**: A few ranks clear old buffers (`ClearCommBuffers_bad`) - **slow path**
6. **T+30ms**: Fast ranks hit MPI_Allreduce, start waiting
7. **T+110ms**: Slow ranks finish Clear, hit MPI_Allreduce
8. **T+110ms**: Collective completes with 80ms+ spread

## Why It Appears Random

The straggler ranks depend on:
1. Current mesh block distribution
2. Physics driving refinement (where to add/remove resolution)
3. Load balancer's cost function decisions
4. Previous timestep's block assignments

As the simulation evolves, different spatial regions become computationally expensive, leading to different redistribution patterns.

## Flows Used

1. `0-probe-counts.yaml` - Initial probe discovery
2. `1-baseline-collectives.yaml` - MPI collective tracking
3. `2-straggler-hunt.yaml` - Kokkos events >1ms + collective raw data

## Recommendations

1. **Optimize ClearCommBuffers_bad**: The function name suggests awareness of the issue. Consider:
   - Lazy clearing (only clear when needed)
   - Amortizing cleanup across timesteps
   - Using a different data structure with cheaper destruction

2. **Load balancer tuning**: Balance the "mesh block count" vs "communication buffer setup cost"

3. **Overlap Clear with communication**: The Clear could potentially happen while waiting for new blocks to arrive

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total ranks | 4096 |
| Ranks with Clear per timestep | 1-11 (0.0-0.3%) |
| Clear duration | 70-84ms |
| Collective spread (anomalous) | 50-115ms |
| Collective spread (normal) | 0.36ms median |
| AMR frequency | ~every 10-11 timesteps |

content.thinking: Now I've provided the complete explanation. The key insight is that it's not just a "map clear" - it's that each CommBuffer destructor potentially calls `MPI_Wait()` which is a blocking operation. With ~200 buffers per rank that need clearing, and some of those having pending MPI operations, the serialized blocking waits accumulate to 70-84ms.

message.content: but can you prove this somehow using ORCA?

content.thinking:
The user wants me to prove that the MPI_Wait calls in the CommBuffer destructor are the cause. I need to capture MPI_Wait events during the Clear operation and show that:

1. MPI_Wait calls happen during the Clear timeframe
2. The number/duration of MPI_Wait calls correlates with Clear duration

Let me create a flow that captures MPI_Wait events (from mpi_messages schema) and correlate them with the Clear timing.

content.text: Good point. Let me capture MPI_Wait events during the Clear window and correlate them.

input.content:
nodes:
  # Raw mpi_collectives - always collect
  mpi_coll_raw:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM mpi_collectives

  # MPI_Wait events - these are the blocking calls in destructor
  mpi_waits:
    from: [mpi_messages]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM mpi_messages
      WHERE probe_name = 'MPI_Wait'

  # Kokkos slow events including Clear
  kokkos_slow:
    from: [kokkos_events]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM kokkos_events
      WHERE dura_ns > 1000000

  # Per-swid stats
  mpi_coll_swid_stats:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        timestep as ts,
        swid,
        probe_name,
        COUNT(*) as cnt,
        MIN(dura_ns) as min_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) as max_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) - MIN(dura_ns) as spread_ns
      FROM mpi_collectives
      GROUP BY timestep, swid, probe_name

sinks:
  mpi_coll_raw_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_raw
    type: parquet
  mpi_waits_pq:
    from: mpi_waits
    type: parquet
  kokkos_slow_pq:
    from: kokkos_slow
    type: parquet
  mpi_coll_swid_stats_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_swid_stats
    type: parquet

message.content: are you going to capture ALL MPI waits? is that the smartest strategy here?

content.thinking:
The user is right - capturing ALL MPI_Wait events would be extremely high volume (we saw 42 million MPI_Wait calls earlier). That's not smart.

A better approach would be to:
1. Filter MPI_Wait by duration - only capture slow ones (>1ms or some threshold)
2. Or filter by the swid range where Clear happens
3. Or aggregate MPI_Wait counts/durations per rank per swid

Let me think about what would actually prove the hypothesis:
- If MPI_Wait calls inside the destructor are causing the 70-84ms Clear duration
- Then during the Clear swid, the straggler ranks should have many/long MPI_Wait calls
- And the total MPI_Wait time should correlate with Clear duration

A smarter strategy would be to capture:
1. MPI_Wait events with duration > some threshold (say 100us or 1ms)
2. Or aggregate stats: per (ts, swid, rank) - count and sum of MPI_Wait durations

Let me propose a better flow.

content.text: You're right - that would be millions of events. Let me be smarter and aggregate MPI_Wait stats per rank/swid to correlate with Clear.

input.content:
nodes:
  # Raw mpi_collectives - always collect
  mpi_coll_raw:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM mpi_collectives

  # MPI_Wait aggregated stats per rank/swid
  # This tells us: how many waits and total wait time per rank in each sync window
  mpi_wait_stats:
    from: [mpi_messages]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        timestep as ts,
        swid,
        rank,
        COUNT(*) as wait_cnt,
        SUM(dura_ns) as total_wait_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) as max_wait_ns
      FROM mpi_messages
      WHERE probe_name = 'MPI_Wait'
      GROUP BY timestep, swid, rank

  # Kokkos slow events including Clear
  kokkos_slow:
    from: [kokkos_events]
    sql: |
      SELECT * FROM kokkos_events
      WHERE dura_ns > 1000000

  # Per-swid collective stats
  mpi_coll_swid_stats:
    from: [mpi_collectives]
    sql: |
      SELECT
        timestep as ts,
        swid,
        probe_name,
        COUNT(*) as cnt,
        MIN(dura_ns) as min_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) as max_dura_ns,
        MAX(dura_ns) - MIN(dura_ns) as spread_ns
      FROM mpi_collectives
      GROUP BY timestep, swid, probe_name

sinks:
  mpi_coll_raw_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_raw
    type: parquet
  mpi_wait_stats_pq:
    from: mpi_wait_stats
    type: parquet
  kokkos_slow_pq:
    from: kokkos_slow
    type: parquet
  mpi_coll_swid_stats_pq:
    from: mpi_coll_swid_stats
    type: parquet

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

# Load data
print("=== LOADING DATA ===")
wait_stats = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_wait_stats/**/*.parquet").collect()
kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()

print(f"MPI wait stats rows: {len(wait_stats)}")
print(f"Kokkos slow rows: {len(kokkos)}")

# Filter to new timestep range
wait_stats = wait_stats.filter(pl.col("ts") >= 466)
kokkos = kokkos.filter(pl.col("timestep") >= 466)

print(f"MPI wait stats (ts>=466): {len(wait_stats)}")
print(f"Kokkos slow (ts>=466): {len(kokkos)}")

# Get Clear events
clear_events = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear")
print(f"Clear events: {len(clear_events)}")

if len(clear_events) == 0:
    print("No Clear events in this range!")
else:
    # For each Clear event, get the MPI_Wait stats for that rank at that swid
    print("\n=== CORRELATING CLEAR WITH MPI_WAIT STATS ===")
    
    for row in clear_events.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(10).iter_rows(named=True):
        ts = row["timestep"]
        swid = row["swid"]
        rank = row["rank"]
        clear_dura_ms = row["dura_ns"] / 1e6
        
        # Get MPI_Wait stats for this rank at this swid
        rank_wait = wait_stats.filter(
            (pl.col("ts") == ts) & 
            (pl.col("swid") == swid) & 
            (pl.col("rank") == rank)
        )
        
        if len(rank_wait) > 0:
            wait_cnt = rank_wait["wait_cnt"][0]
            total_wait_ms = rank_wait["total_wait_ns"][0] / 1e6
            max_wait_ms = rank_wait["max_wait_ns"][0] / 1e6
            print(f"ts={ts}, swid={swid}, rank={rank}: Clear={clear_dura_ms:.1f}ms, MPI_Wait: cnt={wait_cnt}, total={total_wait_ms:.1f}ms, max={max_wait_ms:.2f}ms")
        else:
            print(f"ts={ts}, swid={swid}, rank={rank}: Clear={clear_dura_ms:.1f}ms, NO MPI_WAIT DATA")
    
    # Compare with non-Clear ranks at same swid
    print("\n=== COMPARISON: CLEAR vs NON-CLEAR RANKS AT SAME SWID ===")
    sample_clear = clear_events.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(3)
    
    for row in sample_clear.iter_rows(named=True):
        ts = row["timestep"]
        swid = row["swid"]
        clear_rank = row["rank"]
        
        # All ranks with wait stats at this swid
        swid_waits = wait_stats.filter((pl.col("ts") == ts) & (pl.col("swid") == swid))
        
        if len(swid_waits) > 0:
            # Clear rank stats
            clear_stats = swid_waits.filter(pl.col("rank") == clear_rank)
            other_stats = swid_waits.filter(pl.col("rank") != clear_rank)
            
            print(f"\nts={ts}, swid={swid}:")
            if len(clear_stats) > 0:
                print(f"  Clear rank {clear_rank}: wait_cnt={clear_stats['wait_cnt'][0]}, total_wait={clear_stats['total_wait_ns'][0]/1e6:.1f}ms")
            if len(other_stats) > 0:
                print(f"  Other ranks (n={len(other_stats)}): avg wait_cnt={other_stats['wait_cnt'].mean():.1f}, avg total_wait={other_stats['total_wait_ns'].mean()/1e6:.1f}ms")
EOF

content.thinking:
This is very strong evidence! Look at this:

```
ts=584, swid=3193, rank=2318: Clear=81.9ms, MPI_Wait: cnt=79, total=81.5ms, max=81.46ms
```

The Clear duration (81.9ms) almost exactly matches the total MPI_Wait time (81.5ms)! And there's one max wait of 81.46ms, meaning one very long wait.

And the comparison is even more striking:
- Clear rank 2318: wait_cnt=79, total_wait=81.5ms
- Other 4095 ranks: avg wait_cnt=92, avg total_wait=0.0ms

The Clear ranks have 79-97 MPI_Wait calls that total 78-82ms, while other ranks have ~92 MPI_Wait calls that total ~0ms (instant waits).

This proves the hypothesis:
1. The Clear operation involves ~80-100 MPI_Wait calls
2. These waits take 78-82ms total for Clear ranks
3. Non-Clear ranks have similar wait counts but they complete instantly (0ms)
4. The Clear duration is almost entirely accounted for by MPI_Wait time

The difference is that non-Clear ranks' MPI_Waits complete immediately (their requests are already done), while Clear ranks' MPI_Waits block waiting for pending requests to complete.

Let me also check what the max_wait tells us - the fact that max_wait ≈ total_wait suggests there's ONE very long wait that dominates, not 80 short waits.

```

ts=474, rank=372:  Clear=81.2ms, MPI_Wait total=80.8ms (99.5% match!)
```

And the comparison is stark:
- **Clear rank**: 79 waits, **81.5ms total**
- **Other 4095 ranks**: 92 waits, **0.0ms total** (instant completion!)

The Clear duration is almost entirely MPI_Wait time. Let me dig deeper into what makes these waits slow.

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import polars as pl

parquet_root = "/mnt/ltio/orcajobs/suites/20260108/amr-agg4-r4096-n2000-run1/20_or_disable_paused/parquet"

wait_stats = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/mpi_wait_stats/**/*.parquet").collect()
wait_stats = wait_stats.filter(pl.col("ts") >= 466)

kokkos = pl.scan_parquet(f"{parquet_root}/kokkos_slow/**/*.parquet").collect()
kokkos = kokkos.filter(pl.col("timestep") >= 466)

clear_events = kokkos.filter(pl.col("probe_name") == "region::Mesh::Initialize::BuildAndPost::Clear")
clear_ranks_swids = set(zip(
    clear_events["timestep"].to_list(),
    clear_events["swid"].to_list(),
    clear_events["rank"].to_list()
))

# For Clear swids, compare max_wait vs total_wait
print("=== MAX_WAIT vs TOTAL_WAIT FOR CLEAR RANKS ===")
print("(If max ≈ total, one wait dominates; if max << total, many waits add up)\n")

for row in clear_events.sort("dura_ns", descending=True).head(10).iter_rows(named=True):
    ts = row["timestep"]
    swid = row["swid"]
    rank = row["rank"]
    clear_ms = row["dura_ns"] / 1e6
    
    rank_wait = wait_stats.filter(
        (pl.col("ts") == ts) & 
        (pl.col("swid") == swid) & 
        (pl.col("rank") == rank)
    )
    
    if len(rank_wait) > 0:
        cnt = rank_wait["wait_cnt"][0]
        total_ms = rank_wait["total_wait_ns"][0] / 1e6
        max_ms = rank_wait["max_wait_ns"][0] / 1e6
        ratio = max_ms / total_ms if total_ms > 0 else 0
        print(f"rank={rank}: Clear={clear_ms:.1f}ms, {cnt} waits, total={total_ms:.1f}ms, max={max_ms:.1f}ms, max/total={ratio:.1%}")

# Now look at non-Clear ranks with highest wait times at same swids
print("\n=== NON-CLEAR RANKS WITH HIGHEST WAIT TIMES ===")
# Pick one swid
ts, swid, _ = list(clear_ranks_swids)[0]
swid_waits = wait_stats.filter((pl.col("ts") == ts) & (pl.col("swid") == swid))
top_waiters = swid_waits.sort("total_wait_ns", descending=True).head(20)
print(f"\nts={ts}, swid={swid} - top ranks by total_wait:")
for row in top_waiters.iter_rows(named=True):
    is_clear = (ts, swid, row["rank"]) in clear_ranks_swids
    mark = "CLEAR" if is_clear else ""
    print(f"  rank={row['rank']}: cnt={row['wait_cnt']}, total={row['total_wait_ns']/1e6:.1f}ms, max={row['max_wait_ns']/1e6:.1f}ms {mark}")
EOF
